item.title: 10 Cybersecurity Focus Areas That Actually Move the Needle
item.description: 10 Cybersecurity Focus Areas That Actually Move the Needle

item.title: Abstract Security Named in SACR’s 2025 SDPP Market Guide: The Rise of Security Data Pipelines
item.description: Abstract Security Named in SACR’s 2025 SDPP Market Guide: The Rise of Security Data Pipelines

item.title: 90% of Your Splunk Data Is Helping Attackers, Not Analysts
item.description: Most Splunk environments contain 90%+ data that's never read—driving up costs and creating noise that obscures real threats. Our free app analyzes your indexes and sourcetypes to show you where the waste is and what you can safely cut.

item.title: Abstract and Chris Camacho: Threat intelligence - the next evolution
item.description: Abstract and Chris Camacho: Threat intelligence - the next evolution

item.title: Abstract Joins Torq AMP Alliance Program to Accelerate Agentic SecOps
item.description: Abstract joins the Torq AMP Alliance to accelerate agentic SecOps by combining pipeline-first detection engineering with AI-driven SOC automation.

item.title: Abstract Security + Amazon Security Lake + OCSF = Upgraded Security Data Management!
item.description: Abstract Security + Amazon Security Lake + OCSF = Upgraded Security Data Management!

item.title: Abstract Security Joins Forces with Analytica42 to Supercharge Integration Delivery to Google SecOps and More
item.description: Abstract Security Joins Forces with Analytica42 to Supercharge Integration Delivery to Google SecOps and More

item.title: Abstract Security Partners with Netskope to turn Security Data into Real-Time Decisions
item.description: Abstract Security, the leader in streaming-first security data operations, today announced a partnership with Netskope to provide joint customers the ability to bring detection directly into the data stream.

item.title: Abstract Security Welcomes OmegaBlack To Growing Roster of New Customers
item.description: Abstract Security, building the future of AI enabled security operations, today announced it has added threat intelligence expert OmegaBlack along with several new MSSP customers over the past two months.

item.title: AI Agents and Automated SOCs: Centering End-to-End Workflows for Real Impact
item.description: AI agents can streamline security operations—but only when built on clean data and end-to-end workflows. Learn how automation reduces noise and strengthens the modern SOC.

item.title: Automating macOS Incident Response: DFIR-as-Code in Action Against AppleProcessHub
item.description: Learn how DFIR-as-Code transforms MacOS incident response by automating forensic processes, enhancing efficiency, and reducing human error.

item.title: Breaking the SIEM Mold: Security Data Operations & What’s Next | Live at Black Hat
item.description: Breaking the SIEM Mold: Security Data Operations &amp; What’s Next | Live at Black Hat

item.title: C2 Corner: Achieving Fortune 50 Security With a SMB Budget
item.description: C2 Corner: Achieving Fortune 50 Security With a SMB Budget

item.title: C2 Corner: Detection Doesn’t Fail First: Data Does
item.description: Detection doesn’t fail because teams lack skill. It fails when telemetry, data pipelines, and security engineering aren’t built for modern detection.

item.title: C2 Corner: How I Mentor
item.description: Jim Routh shares the mentoring principles and habits he’s relied on for decades to help CISOs and security leaders grow through ambiguity, responsibility, and trust.

item.title: C2 Corner: Rebooting Security Operations: Mission First, People Always
item.description: Reboot SecOps with integrated tooling, realistic training, AI as a force multiplier, and people-first leadership built for a modern SOC.

item.title: C2 Corner: State of Enterprise Detection Engineering for a Modern SOC
item.description: Explore 2025’s enterprise SOC evolution—data pipelines reshape SIEM economics, CTI drives TTP-based detections, and fundamentals still win the day.

item.title: C2 Corner: Stop Automating the SOC. Start Automating Remediation.
item.description: SOC automation often stops at detection while real fixes lag. Learn how to cut noise, align IT & security, and automate remediation to reduce risk.

item.title: C2 Corner: The Tactician and the Strategist in Cyber Threat Intelligence
item.description: In Cyber Threat Intel, AI is the Tactician. Humans are the Strategists.

item.title: C2 Corner: Why I Mentor
item.description: A reflection from Jim Routh on mentorship, leadership, and paying it forward—sharing lived experience from decades as a CISO and mentor in cybersecurity.

item.title: C2 Corner: Climbing the Mountain Again and Again
item.description: Cybersecurity careers are rarely linear. Explore how structure and perseverance help professionals navigate industry resets and long-term growth.

item.title: Contagious Interview: Evolution of VS Code and Cursor Tasks Infection Chains - Part 1
item.description: ASTRO tracks fresh Contagious Interview evolutions: shortened URLs masking Vercel infrastructure, NVIDIA-impersonating GitHub Gists, and Google Drive payload delivery with a virus-scan bypass. Original research with detection queries and IOCs.

item.title: Contagious Interview: Tracking the VS Code Tasks Infection Vector
item.description: An in-depth analysis of the DPRK-linked Contagious Interview campaign, revealing how malicious VS Code tasks enable silent code execution, payload delivery, and developer compromise via GitHub repos.

item.title: Critical Apache Tika Vulnerability: CVE-2025-66516 Enables XXE Injection
item.description: A critical XXE flaw in Apache Tika (CVE-2025-66516) allows file reads, SSRF, and DoS via malicious PDFs. Learn affected versions, IoCs, and required patches.

item.title: Critical Ivanti EPMM Vulnerabilities: CVE-2026-1281 & CVE-2026-1340
item.description: CISA has added two critical Ivanti EPMM vulnerabilities to the KEV catalog. Learn how CVE-2026-1281 and CVE-2026-1340 are exploited, how to detect attacks via Apache logs, and what to do now.

item.title: Critical MongoDB Vulnerability: CVE-2025-14847 (MongoBleed)
item.description: CVE-2025-14847 (MongoBleed) is a critical MongoDB vulnerability enabling unauthenticated memory disclosure. Learn impact, affected versions, and detection steps.

item.title: Critical React Server Components RCE (CVE-2025-55182): What You Need to Patch Now
item.description: CVE-2025-55182 exposes a deserialization flaw in React Server Components enabling unauthenticated RCE. Review affected versions, IoCs, and mitigation steps.

item.title: DFIR-as-Code: Scaled and Repeatable Incident Response
item.description: DFIR-as-Code: Scaled and Repeatable Incident Response

item.title: Dune Security and Abstract Security Partner to Deliver Real-Time Detection and Automated Risk Reduction
item.description: Dune Security and Abstract Security Partner to Deliver Real-Time Detection and Automated Risk Reduction

item.title: Enabling the MongoDB Logging Firehose
item.description: MongoBleed exposed how quickly MongoDB logging can explode under attack. Learn which logs matter, how verbosity impacts volume, and how to preserve security signal without overwhelming your pipeline.
